prerequisites: A TinyEmail account on an Enterprise plan. The TinyEmail API is available exclusively to Enterprise accounts; non-Enterprise plans must upgrade before they can access the API.
An API key generated from the TinyEmail dashboard (My Account -> API Keys), used to populate the connector's required api_key field.
Awareness of the API rate limit of 60 requests per minute; contact TinyEmail support to request a higher limit.

authMethods.name: API key (X-API-KEY header)
authMethods.summary: TinyEmail uses API key authentication. The connector's api_key secret field holds the key, which TinyEmail sends as the X-API-KEY HTTP header (security scheme type apiKey). Generate the key by logging in at app.tinyemail.com and going to My Account -> API Keys.

setupSteps.title: Confirm Enterprise plan access
setupSteps.body: Verify your TinyEmail account is on an Enterprise plan. The TinyEmail API is restricted to Enterprise accounts; if you are on a non-Enterprise plan you must upgrade before the API and its features become available.

setupSteps.title: Generate a TinyEmail API key
setupSteps.body: Log in to your account at app.tinyemail.com and navigate to My Account -> API Keys to create an API key. This key authenticates all API requests.

setupSteps.title: Configure the connector
setupSteps.body: Provide the generated key in the connector's required api_key field. The connector authenticates by passing this value in the X-API-KEY request header. Keep in mind the default rate limit of 60 requests per minute; contact support@tinyemail.com to request a higher limit if needed.
